lede: A look at October 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sam Konstas and Çağlar Birinci, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Manchester synagogue attack
events.desc: 2 people are killed and at least 4 others injured in an attack on a synagogue in Manchester, UK, during Yom Kippur.

events.title: The Washington Post
events.desc: The Washington Post journalist Jamal Khashoggi is assassinated in the Saudi consulate in Istanbul, Turkey.

events.title: West Nickel Mines School shooting
events.desc: Five Amish girls are murdered in a shooting at a school in Pennsylvania, United States.

events.title: Aeroperú Flight 603
events.desc: Aeroperú Flight 603 crashes into the ocean near Peru, killing all 70 people on board.

events.title: Nguyễn Văn Thiệu
events.desc: South Vietnamese President Nguyen Van Thieu is re-elected in a one-man election.

events.title: Wichita State University football team plane crash
events.desc: An aircraft carrying the Wichita State University football team, administrators, and supporters crashes in Colorado, killing 31 people.

events.title: Thurgood Marshall
events.desc: Thurgood Marshall is sworn in as the first African-American justice of the United States Supreme Court.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Ocean Liner RMS Queen Mary accidentally rams and sinks HMS Curacoa, killing over 300 crewmen aboard Curacoa.

events.title: Stroke
events.desc: Seven days after suffering a "physical collapse" following a speech in Pueblo, Colorado, U.S. president Woodrow Wilson has a catastrophic stroke at the White House, leaving him physically and mentally incapacitated for the remainder of his presidency.

events.title: Nottingham cheese riot
events.desc: The Nottingham Cheese Riot breaks out at the Goose Fair in Nottingham, UK, in response to the excessive cost of cheese.

births.name: Siim Kallas
births.line: Estonian politician, Prime Minister of Estonia

deaths.text: Heinz von Foerster, Austrian-American physicist and philosopher
Alexandru Averescu, Romanian military leader and politician, 24th Prime Minister of Romania
François-Timoléon de Choisy, French historian and author

observances.text: Christian feast day: Denha I of Tikrit (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Memorial of the Holy Guardian Angels
Gandhi's birthday-related observances: International Day of Non-Violence
Independence Day, celebrates the independence of Guinea from France in 1958
